_comment: AMS curated tool registry. Covers MCPs (live system access), skill packs (domain knowledge), and agent configs (cognitive shape). Populated by curate-tools. Initial scaffold entries migrated from mcp-catalogue.json v1.

_types.mcp: Model Context Protocol server — connects AI to live system state
_types.skill-pack: Collection of domain knowledge SKILL.md files — constrains AI to correct patterns
_types.agent-config: Cognitive team composition, guard rails, personas — controls how AI reasons

tools.name: nextjs_call
side_effects: Calls live dev server; may trigger dev-server side effects depending on target

tools.name: browser_eval
side_effects: Launches Playwright browser process; may modify browser state

tools.name: upgrade_nextjs_16
side_effects: Runs codemods that modify source files

tools.name: enable_cache_components
side_effects: Modifies Next.js config and source files

gaps: Requires Next.js 16+ dev server running at /_next/mcp — not applicable to Next.js 15 projects without upgrade
harness.notes: Official Vercel tool. Live introspection of errors, routes, server actions replaces AI guessing from static code. Requires Next.js 16+ dev server. upgrade_nextjs_16 and enable_cache_components are write tools — should not be invoked in audit/review modes.

tools.name: deploy_to_vercel
side_effects: Creates deployment; consumes build minutes; triggers webhooks

tools.name: reply_to_toolbar_thread
side_effects: Posts comment visible to collaborators

tools.name: add_toolbar_reaction
side_effects: Adds reaction visible to collaborators

tools.name: edit_toolbar_message
side_effects: Modifies existing comment

tools.name: change_toolbar_thread_resolve_status
side_effects: Changes thread state

tools.name: get_access_to_vercel_url
side_effects: May record access in Vercel access logs

harness.notes: Official Vercel MCP. deploy_to_vercel is write — must not fire in audit/read-only modes. Toolbar thread tools are visible to collaborators — treat as write.
v1 scaffold migrated from mcp-catalogue. Large surface area — staged CRUD-truthfulness review required. Full curation pending.

tools.name: addCommentToJiraIssue
side_effects: Visible to all watchers; sends notifications

tools.name: createJiraIssue
side_effects: Creates issue; sends notifications

tools.name: transitionJiraIssue
side_effects: Changes status; may trigger workflow automations

tools.name: editJiraIssue
side_effects: Modifies issue; sends notifications

harness.notes: v1 scaffold. Critical for jira-context skill and deliver-work flow. Full curation pending.
v1 scaffold. Vendor-authenticity check needed — community vs official Sitecore. Full curation pending.

install.command: pnpm dlx shadcn@latest mcp init --client claude

tools.name: install_components
side_effects: Writes component files to project; modifies package.json deps

gaps: Installs components but does not teach correct usage patterns (CVA, cn(), Radix data-slot, props.ts). Must be paired with tailwind-radix domain skill or equivalent patterns.md coverage to close harness gap.
harness.notes: install_components is write — adds files and deps. Harness strength is medium: reduces component discovery friction but does not constrain AI to correct composition patterns. Conditional approval: effective only when paired with a skill covering CVA/Radix patterns. Private registries require REGISTRY_TOKEN env var.

install.command: claude mcp add --transport http Storyblok https://mcp.labs.storyblok.com/mcp --header "Authorization: Bearer <token>"

tools.name: execute_mutating
side_effects: Modifies CMS content; triggers webhooks if configured

tools.name: execute_destructive
side_effects: Permanently deletes CMS content; irreversible

tools.name: upload_asset
side_effects: Creates asset record; obtains S3 signed URL

tools.name: upload_asset_finish
side_effects: Finalises asset in Storyblok; makes it addressable

harness.notes: Official Storyblok tool. Discovery-first pattern (search → describe → execute) is itself a harness — forces AI to introspect before acting. Explicit CRUD separation into execute_readonly / execute_mutating / execute_destructive is excellent safety design. RBAC via ?role= query param allows scoping to content_reviewer (read-only), content_editor, or developer. GitHub repo archived Mar 2026 — now fully hosted; no maintenance burden. ToS review recommended before use in commercial projects.

install.command: Copy individual SKILL.md files from .github/plugins/<lang>/skills/<skill-name>/ into target project .claude/skills/

gaps: Version alignment: skills reference specific SDK versions — must verify against target project lockfile before ingestion. Stale skill = accuracy risk.
harness.notes: 174 production-ready skills, 1,158 test scenarios (Ralph Loop). Same SKILL.md format as AMS learn-codebase output — directly composable. Ingestion model: selective copy of individual skills, not bulk import. learn-codebase should cross-reference this pack during ecosystem discovery step rather than regenerating Azure SDK domain knowledge from scratch. friction_cost=3 because per-skill version alignment check is required on ingestion.

install.command: Copy relevant SKILL.md files from vercel-plugin into target project .claude/skills/

gaps: License not confirmed during initial curation — check github.com/vercel/vercel-plugin before production use
harness.notes: Relational knowledge graph + skills for every major Vercel product. Effective pre-built AMS output for Vercel-hosted projects. Pairs well with vercel-mcp and next-devtools-mcp. Ingestion: selective copy of product-specific skills. Full license check required before recommending to commercial projects.

gaps: Knowledge-only — reads stories and docs but cannot execute tests or verify component behaviour. Must be paired with storybook-addon-mcp to close the AI write→test→self-correct loop.
harness.notes: Official Storybook org. Serves component and story knowledge from running Storybook manifests — replaces AI guessing about component API, props, and usage patterns. All tools are pure-read. Requires Storybook dev server running (typically localhost:6006). Pairs with storybook-addon-mcp for test execution. 3M+ monthly downloads. Last commit 2026-04-30.

install.command: npm install -D @storybook/addon-mcp @storybook/addon-vitest vitest && add '@storybook/addon-mcp' to .storybook/main.ts addons array

tools.name: run-story-tests
side_effects: Triggers Vitest test runner via Storybook channel; writes test result artefacts; may update coverage reports

harness.notes: Official Storybook org. Closes the AI development loop: AI writes component → calls run-story-tests → receives structured pass/fail + a11y violations + error details → self-corrects → repeats until green. This is the primary automated verification mechanism for component development. run-story-tests returns { passingStoryCount, failingStoryCount, a11yViolationCount, unhandledErrorCount } plus per-story error details. Conditional approval: requires @storybook/addon-vitest and vitest to be installed and configured — high friction_cost=2 for first-time setup. README is thin (12 lines); implementation is clear from source. Pairs with storybook-mcp for component knowledge context before writing.

tools.name: browser_navigate
side_effects: Changes browser URL; triggers page load; records in history

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
side_effects: Navigates browser history back

tools.name: browser_click
side_effects: Triggers click event; may change page state, submit forms, navigate

tools.name: browser_type
side_effects: Enters text into focused element

tools.name: browser_fill
side_effects: Sets input value directly

tools.name: browser_fill_form
side_effects: Fills multiple form fields; may trigger validation

tools.name: browser_hover
side_effects: Triggers hover/mouseover events; may change UI state

tools.name: browser_drag
side_effects: Triggers drag events; may reorder DOM or state

tools.name: browser_drop
side_effects: Completes drag-drop; modifies page state

tools.name: browser_select_option
side_effects: Changes select element value; triggers change events

tools.name: browser_press_key
side_effects: Triggers keyboard events; may submit forms, navigate, change state

tools.name: browser_resize
side_effects: Changes viewport size; triggers resize events

tools.name: browser_handle_dialog
side_effects: Dismisses or accepts browser dialogs

tools.name: browser_file_upload
side_effects: Attaches file to upload input

tools.name: browser_evaluate
side_effects: Executes arbitrary JavaScript in page context; can cause any side effect depending on script

tools.name: browser_close
side_effects: Closes active browser tab/context

tools.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
side_effects: Runs arbitrary unsafe code in browser; potential for irreversible state changes

gaps: Does not directly run Storybook story-level tests — complements storybook-addon-mcp by covering interactive/DOM assertions that run-story-tests cannot express. Use addon-mcp first; use playwright-mcp for deeper behavioural verification.
harness.notes: Official Microsoft/Playwright team. Enables AI to navigate to Storybook (localhost:6006), interact with components, and assert DOM state — closing the AI write→verify loop for interactive behaviour beyond what Vitest story tests cover. browser_run_code_unsafe is destructive — must never fire in audit/read-only modes. browser_evaluate is read-with-side-effects — treat with caution. Very active: v0.0.74 published 2026-05-06. Last commit 2026-05-06. Apache-2.0.

tools.name: create_item
side_effects: Creates Sitecore item; may trigger workflow and publishing

tools.name: update_item
side_effects: Updates Sitecore item fields; may trigger workflow

tools.name: delete_item
side_effects: Permanently deletes Sitecore item and children unless recycled

tools.name: update_layout
side_effects: Modifies component layout/datasource assignments

tools.name: update_user
side_effects: Modifies Sitecore user account

tools.name: update_role_acl
side_effects: Changes access control; affects all users in role

tools.name: execute_powershell
side_effects: Runs arbitrary PowerShell on Sitecore instance; scope depends on script — can be destructive

tools.name: rebuild_index
side_effects: Triggers full index rebuild; resource-intensive; may affect search availability

gaps: Covers Sitecore server-side APIs (GraphQL, Item Service, PowerShell). Does NOT cover Sitecore Content SDK JavaScript patterns (@sitecore-content-sdk/* composition, component factory, personalization hooks). Pair with a sitecore-xmc-knowledge domain skill generated by learn-codebase to cover the JS SDK layer.
harness.notes: Community tool by Antonytm (EXDST company, 124 public repos, verifiable professional identity). Not official Sitecore — note this to clients. Apache-2.0 in GitHub repo but license field missing from package.json — resolve before production use. 100+ tools covering GraphQL, Item Service, PowerShell, Security, Presentation, Indexing. Last commit 2026-04-11 (active). Docker install available for CI use. SECURITY: execute_powershell runs arbitrary scripts on the Sitecore instance — must be blocked in read-only and audit modes. update_role_acl is sensitive — security posture requires strict human gate. friction_cost=2 because requires running Sitecore instance with Sitecore PowerShell Extensions installed and API credentials. Supersedes sitecore-knowledge-mcp scaffold.

gaps: Tool list not fully documented in README (12-line README in monorepo). Exact tool names (lint_text vs check_code) should be confirmed by connecting to the server and calling tools/list during project setup.
harness.notes: Official ESLint org (OpenJS Foundation). Apache-2.0. Enables AI to lint code as part of the write→verify loop — AI writes code, calls lint, reads violations, self-corrects before reporting done. Supports ESLint v9 flat config (eslint.config.js). Very recently updated (2026-05-06 — 1 day before curation). Conditional approval: tool names unconfirmed due to thin README — verify via tools/list on first install. Re-evaluate tool list after connection. friction_cost=4 because ESLint config is already project-local (low overhead once project has eslint.config.js).
